items.title: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work
items.oneliner: OpenAI bundled GPT-5.6 with ChatGPT Work, pushing the model race toward full-stack task execution, not just better chat.

items.title: Meta released Muse Spark 1.1
items.oneliner: Meta gave Muse Spark 1.1 a 1M-token window and lower pricing, turning long-context agents into a cost war.

items.title: Tencent open-sourced the Hy3 model
items.oneliner: Tencent's Apache-licensed Hy3 packs 295B parameters with 21B active, showing open heavyweight MoE models are still accelerating.

items.title: Researchers chained SQL injection into Apple RCE
items.oneliner: ProjectDiscovery turned SQL injection in Apple's travel portal into remote code execution, a reminder that boring bugs still escalate.

items.title: Patreon moved to block AI crawlers
items.oneliner: Patreon partnered with Cloudflare to block AI training crawlers, signaling content access is becoming an enforceable infrastructure fight.

items.title: AI regulation is now a super PAC battle
items.oneliner: More than $200 million is flowing into AI election spending, so regulation is now being shaped like platform policy.

items.title: ClickHouse scaled PgBouncer past single-core limits
items.oneliner: ClickHouse used so_reuseport and a PgBouncer fleet to hit 4x throughput, making old single-core bottlenecks look optional.

items.title: Mesh LLM spreads inference across existing devices
items.oneliner: Mesh LLM can split models across local machines, which matters if your GPU roadmap is wishful thinking.

items.title: AI datacentres pushed emissions sharply higher
items.oneliner: Microsoft, Amazon, and Google hit 119 million tons CO2e, proving AI infrastructure costs are now physical, not abstract.

items.title: Buf launched a serious Python Protobuf library
items.oneliner: Buf's protobuf-py finally offers full Protobuf support with readable generated code, fixing a long-standing Python developer tax.
